channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Laboratory Network to Boost Disease Detection and Response
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM: Tanzania dey take serious steps to strengthen her national laboratory network so that disease surveillance and readiness for health threats go better. Today, health officials, governmen...

item.title: Man from New York faces charges for attempting to lure Ottawa child online
item.description: Authorities in Ottawa are investigating a case involving a man from New York who has been accused of attempting to lure a child online. The suspect allegedly made contact with the minor through digita...

item.title: Global Reactions to Mojtaba Khamenei’s Appointment as Iran’s New Supreme Leader
item.description: Following the assassination of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli air strikes, Iran’s Assembly of Experts appointed his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, as the new supreme leader. The decision has drawn a str...

item.title: Why Glass Leads as the Ultimate Material for Sustainable and Elegant Packaging
item.description: The article explores how the modern design world is embracing sustainability and timeless aesthetics, with glass emerging as a leading material in eco-friendly packaging. It argues that today’s consum...

item.title: Indigenous Communities in Kenya Face Land Displacement and Rights Challenges Amid Development
item.description: A new report by the Kenya National Commission on Human Rights (KNCHR) has highlighted serious challenges facing indigenous communities in Kenya. According to the report, communities such as the Ogiek,...

item.title: Why Independent Coffee Shops Share a Surprisingly Uniform Look
item.description: Independent coffee shops in North America are designed to offer unique, local experiences and ethical, specialty beverages. Yet, a recent study reveals that these cafes often share a strikingly simila...

item.title: Ayra Starr Explains Inspiration Behind Her Latest Song 'Where Do We Go'
item.description: Nigerian singer Ayra Starr don open up say her new single 'Where Do We Go' na because of one secret relationship wey she dey. For interview with 'Way Up With Angela Yee,' Ayra talk say the matter na '...

item.title: Allegations of Regional False Flag Operations in the Gulf and Europe
item.description: According to the report, Israel is allegedly increasing operations described as false flag attacks targeting several countries in the Gulf region and parts of Europe. Incidents mentioned include attac...

item.title: Understanding Panic Buying: Why Australians Stockpile During Crises
item.description: Amid recent fuel price spikes and energy shortages, Australians have been flocking to petrol stations, echoing the panic buying seen during early COVID-19 lockdowns. Research from 2020 shows that pani...

item.title: Lok Sabha rejects no-confidence motion against Speaker Om Birla amid opposition protests
item.description: In a significant parliamentary development, the Lok Sabha on Wednesday rejected a no-confidence motion seeking the removal of Speaker Om Birla. The resolution was defeated by a voice vote, despite vig...

item.title: Comelec expects to declare winner of Antipolo 2nd District special elections on March 15
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) announced that it aims to proclaim the winning candidate of the special elections for the second district of Antipolo City on March 15, 2026, a day after the poll...

item.title: Rostam Azizi Becomes Majority Shareholder in Nation Media Group
item.description: Rostam Azizi, a prominent businessman from Tanzania, has officially acquired majority ownership of Nation Media Group (NMG), buying a 54.08% controlling stake from the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Devel...

item.title: Innovative Lunar New Year Packaging Turns Into a Horse-Racing Board Game
item.description: Vietnamese design studio Witta Twist has created a visually striking and multifunctional project for Web3 company Ninety Eight, blending package design and interactive play in one concept. Named 'The ...

item.title: Guardian film editor Catherine Shoard answers readers' questions on the 2026 Oscars
item.description: Guardian film editor Catherine Shoard addresses readers' queries regarding the 98th Academy Awards, reflecting on trends, campaigning, and notable nominees. She observes that the Oscars often fail to ...

item.title: Former RCMP Officer Believes 1978 Arctic Satellite Assignment Contributed to Cancer Diagnosis
item.description: A former Royal Canadian Mounted Police officer has shared that his recent cancer diagnosis may be connected to a 1978 assignment in the Canadian Arctic, where he was tasked with guarding a crashed Sov...

item.title: Manufacturers Association cautions on illegal recycling of company-owned bottles and crates
item.description: The Manufacturers Association of Nigeria (MAN) don issue warning against the illegal destruction and recycling of returnable packaging materials wey belong to beverage companies. Dis warning come afte...

item.title: Nadra updates database by cancelling CNICs of deceased individuals
item.description: The National Database and Registration Authority (Nadra) has taken steps to update the national registration system by cancelling 4.2 million Computerised National Identity Cards (CNICs) of people rep...

item.title: Kenyan Environmentalist Truphena Muthoni to Attempt World Record for Most Trees Hugged in One Hour
item.description: Truphena Muthoni, a 22-year-old Kenyan environmentalist, is gearing up for another remarkable challenge in Brazil. She is aiming to set a new Guinness World Record by hugging 1,172 trees within a sing...

item.title: Keeping Cats Indoors or in Safe Outdoor Enclosures Increases Longevity
item.description: A recent study highlights that allowing domestic cats to roam freely significantly increases their risk of injury and death. In Australia alone, domestic cats kill hundreds of millions of native anima...

item.title: MEA Confirms Two Indians Killed and One Missing in Merchant Vessel Attacks in Middle East
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) has confirmed that two Indian nationals have lost their lives, and another is missing after merchant vessels carrying them were attacked amid the ongoing conflic...
